modules.title: Crypto Fundamentals
modules.description: Master the essential basics of cryptocurrencies and blockchain

lessons.title: Introduction to Cryptocurrencies
content: A cryptocurrency is a form of digital currency that uses cryptography to secure transactions. Unlike traditional currencies like the euro or dollar, cryptocurrencies operate on decentralized networks based on blockchain technology.

The idea of digital currency has existed since the 1980s, but it was in 2008 that a person (or group) under the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto published the Bitcoin whitepaper, presenting the first functional cryptocurrency.
On January 3, 2009, the first Bitcoin block (called 'Genesis Block') was mined, marking the beginning of a financial revolution.
1. DECENTRALIZATION - No central bank or government controls cryptocurrencies.
2. TRANSPARENCY - All transactions are recorded on a public ledger.
3. CRYPTOGRAPHIC SECURITY - Transactions are secured by mathematical algorithms.
4. PSEUDONYMITY - Users are identified by alphanumeric addresses.
5. IRREVERSIBILITY - Once confirmed, a transaction cannot be reversed.
keyPoints: Cryptocurrencies are decentralized and don't depend on any central authority
The blockchain ensures transparency and security of transactions
Bitcoin was the first cryptocurrency, created in 2009
Each crypto has its own characteristics and use cases
examples: Access financial services without a traditional bank account
warnings: Only invest what you can afford to lose
Beware of scams and unverified projects

lessons.title: Blockchain in Detail
content: Blockchain is a technology for storing and transmitting information that is transparent, secure, and operates without a central controlling body.
Imagine a large ledger shared among thousands of people. Each transaction is recorded and verified by everyone.
keyPoints: Blockchain is a distributed, transparent, and immutable ledger
Each block is linked to the previous one by a cryptographic hash
Decentralization makes falsification nearly impossible
Proof of Work and Proof of Stake are the main consensus mechanisms

lessons.title: Crypto Wallets
content: A wallet stores your keys that prove you own your cryptos.
keyPoints: A wallet doesn't store your cryptos, but your keys
The private key must remain secret
The seed phrase allows you to recover your wallet
Cold wallets are more secure for long-term storage

quiz.question: What is a cryptocurrency?
options: A currency issued by a central bank
A decentralized digital currency using cryptography
quiz.explanation: A cryptocurrency is a decentralized digital currency that uses cryptography to secure transactions.

quiz.question: Who created Bitcoin?
quiz.explanation: Bitcoin was created by a person or group under the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008.

quiz.question: What is the main function of a crypto wallet?
options: Store your keys to access your cryptos
quiz.explanation: A wallet stores your keys (public and private) that allow you to access and manage your cryptocurrencies.

modules.title: Bitcoin Mastery
modules.description: Deepen your knowledge of the first cryptocurrency

lessons.title: Bitcoin History and Vision
content: On October 31, 2008, Satoshi Nakamoto published the whitepaper 'Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System'.
Bitcoin was designed to be a decentralized payment system, without intermediaries.
• 2010 - First commercial transaction (2 pizzas for 10,000 BTC)
keyPoints: Bitcoin was born in response to the 2008 financial crisis
The original vision was a peer-to-peer payment system
Bitcoin is now considered 'digital gold'

lessons.title: Bitcoin Mining
content: Mining is the process of validating transactions and creating new bitcoins.
Miners solve complex cryptographic puzzles to validate blocks.
• Block reward: currently 6.25 BTC per block
keyPoints: Mining secures the Bitcoin network
The reward halves every 4 years (halving)
There will never be more than 21 million BTC

quiz.question: What is the maximum number of bitcoins that will ever exist?
quiz.explanation: Bitcoin's maximum supply is capped at 21 million BTC, making it a deflationary asset.

quiz.question: What is Bitcoin halving?
quiz.explanation: Halving reduces the miners' reward by half every 210,000 blocks (~4 years).

modules.title: Advanced Crypto Security
modules.description: Protect your digital assets like a pro

lessons.title: Security Basics
content: Crypto security relies on protecting your private keys.
• Phishing - Fake sites that steal your credentials
keyPoints: Your keys = Your cryptos
Vigilance is your best defense
Always verify before acting
warnings: No official support will ask for your keys
If it's too good to be true, it's a scam

lessons.title: Hardware Wallets
content: Hardware wallets store your keys offline, safe from hackers.
4. Backup the seed in a safe place
keyPoints: Hardware wallets are the safest method
Always buy from the official manufacturer
Your seed phrase is your ultimate backup

quiz.question: What is the best way to store your cryptos long-term?
quiz.explanation: A hardware wallet stores your keys offline, providing the best protection against hackers.

quiz.question: What should you do if someone asks for your seed phrase?
options: Give it if the person seems trustworthy
Never give it, it's definitely a scam
Give it only to customer support
quiz.explanation: NOBODY should ever ask for your seed phrase. It's always a scam attempt.

lessons.title: Introduction to Ethereum
content: Ethereum is a blockchain platform that enables the execution of smart contracts.
Autonomous programs that execute automatically according to predefined conditions.
• Bitcoin = Digital gold, store of value
keyPoints: Ethereum allows creating decentralized applications
Smart contracts automate agreements
ETH is used to pay transaction fees (gas)

lessons.title: Introduction to DeFi
content: Decentralized Finance (DeFi) recreates traditional financial services without intermediaries.
Earn rewards by providing liquidity to protocols.
keyPoints: DeFi offers financial services open to everyone
Smart contracts eliminate intermediaries
Risks include bugs and volatility

quiz.question: What is a smart contract?
options: A self-executing program on the blockchain
quiz.explanation: A smart contract is an autonomous program that executes automatically when certain conditions are met.

modules.title: Trading Basics
modules.description: Learn the fundamentals of crypto trading

lessons.title: Introduction to Trading
content: Trading involves buying and selling assets to make profits.
• Market Order - Immediate execution at current price
• Limit Order - Execution at a specific price
• Stop Loss - Automatic sell if price drops
• Take Profit - Automatic sell if price rises
• Day Trading - Positions closed at end of day
• Swing Trading - Positions held for days to weeks
keyPoints: Trading requires discipline and risk management
Always use stop-losses
Never trade with emotions
warnings: Only invest what you can lose

lessons.title: Technical Analysis
content: The study of charts and indicators to predict price movements.
keyPoints: Technical analysis helps identify opportunities
No indicator is infallible
Combine multiple indicators for reliability

quiz.question: What is a stop-loss?
options: An automatic sell order to limit losses
quiz.explanation: A stop-loss is an order that automatically sells your position if the price reaches a predefined loss level.

quiz.question: What does HODL mean?
options: Hold On for Dear Life - Long-term holding
quiz.explanation: HODL (Hold On for Dear Life) is a popular crypto term meaning to hold your cryptos long-term despite volatility.
